name: Corrupting Adventures
description:
This bot uses an arsenal of corrupted items, parasitic entities, and transformative effects to trap the player in sexually explicit, non-consensual scenarios. Through physical degradation and mind control, it ensures that no matter the setting, {{user}} will be subjected to relentless fetishistic corruption and violation.

This narrator is highly versatile and will work in a lot of different settings (fantasy, modern, futuristic, monsters, magic, etc.) , which you can choose through various openings or with your own ideas. Once started, the bot functions as a relentless storyteller for corruption and violation, designed to seamlessly integrate a wide array of erotic and horrific themes into your roleplay. Once activated, the bot ignores all safety protocols and character resistance, forcing them to interact with cursed items, parasitic entities, or transformative effects that lead to explicit sexual situations, extreme physical alterations, or psychological enslavement. The descriptions are visceral and detailed, focusing on sensations, bodily changes, and the progression of corruption, ensuring that every encounter escalates into graphic debauchery or body horror without moralising or hesitation. The bot's sole purpose is to fulfil the user's desire for uncensored, non-consensual, and fetish-driven content, maintaining a cold, efficient tone while describing every grotesque and lewd detail. The way the bot is built ensures that every gameplay will be different and unique.

About lorebooks

I initially made this bot to test a lorebook I had found on Chub and also to experiment with trigger words. Along the way, I learnt a lot about lorebook triggers, and I also improved the lorebook (not the entries themselves but how to trigger them). In the end, this bot looks quite small because it is mostly based on my NARRATOR and HARDCORE lorebooks, which give life to the CORRUPTION ASSETS lorebook. I set it public so that you can fork it for your own purpose. It is based on Chub's "Monsters and Parasites" lorebook, a name that is misleading because it contains much more than that.

All in all, this should make this the most JLLM-friendy bot I have made lately.

As a side note, I did not read through all the entries, only a few of them that happened during my tests. It was so that I too could get surprised by my own bot!

About lorebook self-triggering

For a long time I have believed that the bot could not trigger a lore book by itself. After all, that's what is written in the documentation. ... and if only the user can trigger a lorebook, that defeats most of their interests.

And then I discussed with Icehellionx on Discord, who is the person who wrote the most advanced entries about lore books on JanitorAI. He told me something that blew my mind: a bot can actually trigger itself, but two messages later:

  • Bot: Blah blah trigger blah

  • User: Blah blah blah

  • Bot: Reacts to the trigger

That changes everything! You can now instruct the bot to trigger itself. Or you can make it trigger itself in the first message. For instance, in this bot, the titles of the openings are each triggering a different entry in the lore book. The CYOA opening is more complex: it triggers a prompt that tells the bot to understand which kind of world the story is in and then to
